title: ICE Agents Shoot Man Six Times in California Traffic Stop; Eyewitness Contradicts Officials
excerpt: ICE agents shot Carlos Ivan Mendoza Hernández six times during a traffic stop near Interstate 5 in Patterson, California. An eyewitness corroborated the victim's account that at least one agent fired before the car moved, contradicting ICE Director Todd Lyons's claim that the man had 'weaponized his vehicle.'
content:
On April 7, 2026, multiple ICE agents shot Carlos Ivan Mendoza Hernández six times—including in the jaw and arm—during a traffic stop near Interstate 5 in Patterson, California, in what became another contested ICE use-of-force incident.

  • ICE Director Todd Lyons stated the man was a gang member wanted in connection with a murder in El Salvador and that agents fired because he 'weaponized his vehicle,' attempting to run them over. The man's attorney disputed both the gang claim and the account of events as contradicted by court records.
  • Dashboard camera videos from passing vehicles, an eyewitness account, and the victim's own statements all raised questions about the sequence of events. An eyewitness told Stocktonia News that 'at least one agent opened fire unprovoked,' before the car moved.
  • Mendoza Hernández was shot six times, suffering wounds so severe that 'his bones were sticking out' of his arm. He was taken directly from intensive care—after his fourth surgery—into FBI custody, without his attorney's knowledge of his location.
  • As with other high-profile ICE shooting incidents in 2025–2026, federal officials claimed self-defense while video and eyewitness accounts contradicted those claims—repeating a pattern documented in the Good, Pretti, and Martinez shootings.
mediaPlaceholder: Dashboard camera footage from passing vehicles showing ICE agents surrounding Mendoza Hernández's vehicle at the moment of the shooting
